gloss: hard-heartedness, i.e. (specially), destitution of (spiritual) perception
of similar spirit
forward in spirit, i.e. predisposed; neuter (as noun) alacrity
a dæmon or supernatural spirit (of a bad nature)
ruin or loss (physical, spiritual or eternal)
little-spirited, i.e. faint-hearted
co-spirited, i.e. similar in sentiment
a hut or temporary residence, i.e. (figuratively) the human body (as the abode of the spirit)
to inter in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to assimilate spiritually (to Christ by a sepulture as to sin)
to crush together, i.e. (figuratively) to dispirit
sleep, i.e. (figuratively) spiritual torpor
to have faith (in, upon, or with respect to, a person or thing), i.e. credit; by implication, to entrust (especially one's spiritual well-being to Christ)
to be frank in utterance, or confident in spirit and demeanor

graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
(properly concrete) a (mere) show ("phantasm"), i.e. spectre

from the same as אָב (apparently through the idea of prattling a father's name); properly, a mumble, i.e. a water skin (from its hollow sound); hence a necromancer (ventriloquist, as from a jar); bottle, familiar spirit.
from חָרַשׁ; deaf (whether literally or spiritual); deaf.
from נָשַׁם; a puff, i.e. wind, angry or vital breath, divine inspiration, intellect. or (concretely) an animal; blast, (that) breath(-eth), inspiration, soul, spirit.
from קָצַר; shortness (of spirit), i.e. impatience; anguish.

from רוּחַ; wind; by resemblance breath, i.e. a sensible (or even violent) exhalation; figuratively, life, anger, unsubstantiality; by extension, a region of the sky; by resemblance spirit, but only of a rational being (including its expression and functions); air, anger, blast, breath, [idiom] cool, courage, mind, [idiom] quarter, [idiom] side, spirit(-ual), tempest, [idiom] vain, (whirl-) wind(-y).
